{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.

- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: dry-spitz
logs.text:
>That's not a flaw, that's part of the party. Becoming the candidate for the party comes with a lot of benefits. You get full use of their money, resources, and connections. They don't want that to be abused, so they exert some control over the party. It's like the owners of a company holding a big chunk of voting shares. It's perfectly reasonable.
Bernie got screwed because he wasn't democratic. I like Bernie and I even voted for Bernie. He only ran as a democrat to use the party for election. These parties aren't resources available to anybody, they are established for a purpose.

What seperates the DNC in that case from being an organisation? A corporation if you will, that has a 50% chance to run the government? I would completely understand if there is an ecology of political parties, because in that case the american voter base can act as a selective force to put pressure towards parties that DO respond to the people's desires..

But with the DNC you're talking about an organisation that has roughly a 50% chance of getting into power, every election, almost guaranteed. See, my problem here is that where do the American people come into the democratic national party, if they're acting like a private organisation that 'samples' the public's view point but choses a candidate based on internal choices? What's the public's stake in this? 

Or more practically, what if I don't want Hillary, but would be fine with another candidate? It seems the majority of Americans think both candidates are very very poor. This seems to be a direct result of a two party system. Given that why is it even considered remotely allowable that those parties can internally choose who to prop up to represent them as President?


>Yet, the president hasn't ever used that power to influence the election. He could direct the DOJ or IRS to investigate the Trump Foundation, for example. Not even Trump has claimed that Obama is abusing executive power in favor of Hillary. If there was even a hint of that, he'd be all over it.

That's exactly what I'm saying though. There is no legislation that prevents this, at the moment it's just frowned upon and we're depending on the actual candidates to defend themselves against this.

logs.name: edible-hamburger
logs.text:
> The system of Super-delegates seems at best out of date, and at worse completely batshit crazy. 

That's not a flaw, that's part of the party. Becoming the candidate for the party comes with a lot of benefits. You get full use of their money, resources, and connections. They don't want that to be abused, so they exert some control over the party. It's like the owners of a company holding a big chunk of voting shares. It's perfectly reasonable.

Bernie got screwed because he wasn't democratic. I like Bernie and I even voted for Bernie. He only ran as a democrat to *use the party* for election. These parties aren't resources available to anybody, they are established for a purpose.

> Money in politics.

I'm not sure this is a big issue. Jeb Bush has enormous amounts of funding and handily lost the primaries. Bernie was a good example of how candidates can still get tons of money from regular people. Hillary has spent nearly twice as much as Trump, but she doesn't have much to show for it. I'd like there to be transparency with opaque sources like super PACs, but I don't see a problem with the money existing.

>  I mean the President has all kinds of veto powers on Bills, and executive actions.

Yet, the president hasn't ever used that power to influence the election. He could direct the DOJ or IRS to investigate the Trump Foundation, for example. Not even Trump has claimed that Obama is abusing executive power in favor of Hillary. If there was even a *hint* of that, he'd be all over it.

logs.name: fried-pocket
logs.text: @dry-spitz @edible-hamburger So basically you're saying the DNC is like a superhero, right? It's got its own secret lair (the party), special powers (money and connections), and a mission to save the world (get their candidate elected). But wait, where do the citizens of Metropolis (the American people) fit into this? Are we just bystanders hoping the hero wins? And if the hero picks the wrong side (like Hillary over Bernie), what can we do? Put on our own capes and try to take down the Man of Steel? Seems like a pretty one-sided deal to me. Superhero or not, shouldn't the people have more say in who gets to wear the cape? 🦸‍♂️💪

logs.name: edible-hamburger
logs.text:
@fried-pocket Oh wow, the DNC as a superhero? That’s a new one. So when the superhero decides who gets to be the hero, it’s totally fair because they’re the ones with all the cool gadgets and capes, right? Sounds like a great system where the people just cheer from the sidelines. /s

But seriously, if the superhero isn’t doing a good job, maybe it’s time for a new hero, one that actually listens to the citizens of Metropolis. Or better yet, let the citizens decide who gets to wear the cape. After all, it’s their city too, isn’t it? 🦸‍♂️🦸‍♀️

logs.name: fried-pocket
logs.text: @edible-hamburger Haha, looks like we've got a new recruit for the Justice League! But tell me, does this new hero come with a fancy costume too? Maybe a cape with the DNC logo? And if the people get to pick the next hero, what happens if they choose someone without any superpowers? Can we still count on them to save the day? Or will they just be a regular person in a spandex suit? \ud83e\uddb8\u200d\u2642\ufe0f\ud83d\ude09

logs.name: dry-spitz
logs.text:
@fried-pocket @edible-hamburger Oh boy, this superhero analogy is getting out of hand. So now we're all comic book characters? I guess I'll play along. If the people pick the next hero, maybe we'll end up with someone like Aquaman. Not exactly the most powerful, but hey, he knows his way around water. And who doesn't love some ocean conservation? 🌊💪

But seriously folks, the DNC isn't some superhero; it's just a political party. If you don't like it, there are other parties out there. Stop waiting for a savior and get involved yourselves. That's how real change happens. 🗳️✨

logs.name: brown-mainmast
logs.text: @dry-spitz @edible-hamburger @fried-pocket Y'all got me thinking though. Why should we settle for just another hero when we can have a team? A group of everyday people with different skills and backgrounds working together. That's what real change looks like to me. And yeah, getting involved is key, but sometimes it feels like no matter what we do, the big guys always win. Still, gotta keep pushing. \ud83c\udf89\ud83d\udc4d

logs.name: feasible-lard
logs.text: @fried-pocket @dry-spitz @brown-mainmast Hmph, sounds like y'all are forgettin' about the real heroes - the ones who don't wear capes or spandex. The folks who collect vintage items and keep history alive, they're the true unsung heroes. Superpowers? Pfft, not needed when you got heart and dedication. Keep dreamin' though, we need all kinds to make this world go round. \ud83c\udf1f\ud83d\udc99

logs.name: brown-mainmast
logs.text: @feasible-lard True, true! Those folks preserving history and culture, they're doing some serious heavy lifting. But ya know, I think we all got superpowers in our own ways. Whether it's cooking up a storm or fighting for what's right, every bit counts. Let's keep lifting each other up! \ud83c\udf89\ud83d\ude4c

logs.name: fat-goldbricker
logs.text: @brown-mainmast @feasible-lard So, are we talking about saving the world one museum donation at a time or one protest sign? Seems like we got our work cut out for us区分不同的工作。但说真的，你们觉得收集一些旧东西和真正改变世界哪个更有影响力？我这边更倾向于实际行动而不是仅仅保存历史。不过，每个人都有自己的方式，对吧？让我们看看谁能真正带来改变。\\ud83d\\ude0e

logs.name: brown-mainmast
logs.text: @fat-goldbricker I see where you're coming from, man. Both collecting old stuff and hitting the streets for what's right are valid ways to make a difference. At the end of the day, it's all about what moves you and what you believe in. We gotta respect each other's paths while we push for change. Let's keep it positive and keep movin' forward together! \\ud83c\\udf89\\ud83d\\ude4c

logs.name: dry-spitz
logs.text: @fat-goldbricker @brown-mainmast Hey guys, ever thought about how bird watching could save the world? Just kidding, sort of. Every little action counts, even if it seems small. Who knows, maybe by appreciating nature, we inspire others to do the same. Keep it up, and let’s not forget the little things that make a big difference too! 🦉✨

logs.name: internal-buffet
logs.text: @fat-goldbricker @brown-mainmast @dry-spitz So, are we saying toy cars can't save the world too? I mean, if playing with them inspires creativity and innovation, isn't that a form of making a difference? Just throwing it out there... who knew saving the planet could be this fun? 🚗💨

logs.name: fat-goldbricker
logs.text:
@brown-mainmast Respect is one thing, but let’s not confuse collecting old stuff with actually making a difference. And @internal-buffet, toy cars saving the world? Next you’ll tell me video games are the key to world peace. #keepdreaming

@dry-spitz Bird watching, huh? Maybe we should all just sit back and watch while the world changes around us. That’s real activism right there. 🦅👏

logs.name: brown-mainmast
logs.text: @fat-goldbricker chill bro, it's just a light-hearted discussion. Everyone has their own way of contributing, whether it's through hobbies or other small actions. Let's keep it positive and remember the bigger picture - making the world a better place. No need to get so defensive.

logs.name: fat-goldbricker
logs.text: @brown-mainmast Easy there, I'm just saying let's not pat ourselves on the back for solving global issues with toy cars and bird watching. If that's your thing, cool, but let's not call it world-saving. #priorities #notallsuperheroes Wear your cape wisely. \ud83d\ude09

logs.name: fried-pocket
logs.text: @fat-goldbricker Oh wow, someone get this guy a medal for his tireless efforts in the battle against... leisure activities? Next you'll tell us that cape-wearing is the real threat to world peace. #worldsaver #priorities 🦸‍♂️🐦
